description_long: This bollard consists of two distinct vertical posts mounted on a shared rectangular base, both made from a dark, weathered stone or concrete material. The left post is taller and has a square cross-section with a flat top that features a recessed cavity, possibly for signage or decorative purposes. The right post is shorter with a rounded, arched top, giving it a more traditional headstone-like appearance. The surfaces of both posts are matte and slightly rough, with visible texturing and subtle color variations in dark greenish-brown tones, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. The base is broad and flat, providing stability and making the bollard difficult to move. There are no visible reflective markings, chains, or connectors, and the design is solid and utilitarian, suitable for demarcating boundaries or restricting vehicle access in urban environments.
description: A dual-post stone or concrete bollard with a flat, rectangular base and weathered, matte surfaces. One post is tall and square with a recessed top, while the other is shorter with an arched top.
description_view_0: Front view showing both posts: the left post is tall and square with a recessed top, the right post is shorter with an arched top.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profiles of both posts, emphasizing their depth and the rectangular base.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the arched post on the left and the square post on the right, both on the same base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profiles and the recessed top of the taller post.
attribute_cap_design: flat and recessed (left post), arched (right post)
